Tutorial benchmarking sigma-point, Monte Carlo, linearisation, and bootstrap propagation of parameter uncertainty through Baranyi-style growth models — focuses on propagation accuracy rather than freq
Compares experimental designs for estimating growth-model parameters and quantifies the resulting parameter uncertainties.

Demonstrates that experimental detection limits and censoring induce systematic bias in fitted growth-model parameters — a misspecification of the data-generating process rather than the deterministic
Propagates parameter uncertainty from Gompertz-style spoilage fits to shelf-life predictions but does not validate the coverage of the resulting intervals.
Robust estimation method for primary kinetic models when data are noisy/censored; discusses uncertainty but not coverage.
Hierarchical/multilevel growth-curve models for separating biological from measurement variability; presents parameter intervals but does not measure their frequentist coverage.
Classic ComBase-era growth/survival model under fluctuating conditions; provides prediction uncertainty but no coverage validation.
Early ComBase-style validation study comparing predictions to independent data; uses RMSE-type metrics rather than CI coverage.
One of the earliest applications of Bayesian MCMC to bacterial growth-curve fitting; reports credible intervals but does not check frequentist coverage or sensitivity to prior/misspecification.
Bayesian hierarchical model of Listeria growth with variability components; gives credible intervals on growth rates without coverage diagnostics.
Bayesian fit of Jameson-effect competition model to growth data with posterior parameter intervals — relevant Bayesian-growth-curve antecedent but no coverage analysis.
Bayesian competition growth model with prior sensitivity discussion; no coverage assessment.
Hierarchical Bayesian modelling of censored contamination counts; emphasises uncertainty quantification rather than calibration.
Bayesian growth-rate estimation showing informative priors improve inference on differences — a sensitivity-to-prior result relevant to our discussion.
Bayesian primary-model fits to phenotype microarray growth curves; quantifies posterior parameter uncertainty.
Uses OED on non-isothermal Baranyi fits to reduce parameter standard errors; assumes correct model.
Compares D-/E-optimal and standard DoE for growth-model estimation; reports parameter precision (CV, SE) without coverage.
Foundational shelf-life model with empirical validation against independent challenge tests; classic ComBase-era validation paradigm.
Standard reference establishing the profile-likelihood approach to practical identifiability and CI construction in nonlinear ODE models — directly applicable to growth-curve fits.
Constructs prediction bands for ODE trajectories via profile-likelihood integration; directly applicable to growth-curve predictive intervals.
Develops Monte Carlo profile CI methodology for noisy partially-observed dynamic systems with proof of nominal coverage under regularity.
Studies finite-sample LRT calibration for nonlinear ODE models and shows when chi-square approximation fails — directly bears on our claim that asymptotic CIs miscover at small N.
Demonstrates profile-likelihood identifiability and CI coverage for biological growth/invasion PDE/ODE models with simulation studies — close in spirit to our work but for cell-invasion models.
Simulation study of identifiability and confidence-region coverage for biological-ODE fits; shows how unidentifiability inflates intervals.
Algorithm for profile-likelihood CIs in nonlinear dose-response (structurally similar to growth) with simulation-based coverage check.
Classic reference for higher-order likelihood (Skovgaard/modified profile) CIs in nonlinear regression including dose-response curves — methodological backbone for any small-sample nonlinear CI work.
The standard textbook reference for inference in nonlinear regression including linearisation Wald intervals, likelihood-based intervals, and curvature diagnostics.
Standard textbook covering intrinsic/parameter-effects curvature, profile traces, and the bias of Wald CIs in nonlinear regression — direct intellectual ancestor of any growth-curve CI critique.
Definitive case-study treatment of higher-order asymptotic CIs in nonlinear and non-Gaussian models including nlreg software — relevant to small-sample growth-curve fits.
Foundational PSIS-LOO paper introducing fast and stable Bayesian model evaluation with importance-weight diagnostics; essential citation for any modern Bayesian inference workflow.
Technical foundation of PSIS — generalized-Pareto tail fitting to stabilize importance weights with k-hat diagnostic for unreliable weights.

Defines SBC: rank statistics under data simulated from the prior should be uniform if the posterior sampler is correctly calibrated — the canonical way to detect mis-calibration of Bayesian intervals.

Constructs Bayesian posteriors that target the pseudo-true parameter under model misspecification with asymptotic frequentist coverage — directly relevant theoretical backdrop for our misspec results.
Develops a model-free theory of parametric regression under misspecification including sandwich variance estimators — frequentist counterpart to the Hoff-Wakefield Bayesian sandwich.
Establishes safe-Bayes (learning-rate-tempered) posteriors that concentrate around the KL-projection under misspecification — foundational generalised-Bayes reference.
Theoretical convergence rates for generalised Bayes under misspecification across loss functions; underpins the safe-Bayes coverage story.
Generalised predictive calibration algorithm that tunes the learning rate so credible intervals achieve nominal coverage under misspecification.
Benchmarks learning-rate tuning methods for safe-Bayes using coverage probability as a primary metric — exactly the kind of calibration diagnostic we use.
Post-hoc isotonic calibration of regression credible intervals to nominal coverage — a recalibration approach broadly relevant to our misspecification fix.
Extends SBC to validate posterior inference conditional on the observed data — directly applicable to ODE-/growth-curve calibration checks.
Refines SBC by using data-dependent test quantities; increases sensitivity to subtle miscalibration relevant to nonlinear/ODE fits.
PSIS-based approximate LFO-CV for time-series models — directly applicable to evaluating Bayesian growth-curve predictive performance.
Bayesian model stacking under M-open (i.e., misspecified) settings using PSIS weights; addresses misspecification by averaging rather than picking one model.
Gibbs/safe-Bayes posterior framework for PDE-based inverse problems with model misspecification — methodologically analogous to misspecified ODE growth fits.
Bayesian posteriors that are asymptotically calibrated to the pseudo-true parameter under misspecification — theoretical justification for sandwich-style adjustments to Bayesian CIs.
Risk-management application of predictive-microbiology models; demonstrates use but not statistical calibration.
Profile-likelihood-based parameter identifiability and CI construction for biological diffusion models — methodological cousin to growth-curve fits.
Uses 2D profile-likelihoods for OED in ODE models; closely related toolkit to ours.
Software-focused implementation of profile-likelihood CIs for systems-biology ODE models with explicit identifiability handling.

uncertainty_reported: Acknowledges that the default mechanism for producing CIs in the dominant pop-PK tool routinely fails, forcing modelers to bootstrap or skip CIs.
notes: NONMEM covariance step failure is common; failure means standard errors and confidence intervals on parameters are unavailable or unreliable.

uncertainty_reported: Sensitivity analysis is recommended but a quantitative uncertainty bound on PBPK predictions is not strictly mandated; risk-based credibility framework being developed under ICH M15.
notes: FDA PBPK Format and Content Guidance: sensitivity analysis is required for key/uncertain parameters; reviewers consistently emphasize "pervasive uncertainty" in PBPK submissions. Acceptance is case-by

uncertainty_reported: Reporting of SEs/CIs is required but the guideline does not prescribe a particular calibration check for those CIs.
notes: EMA PBPK reporting guideline requires sensitivity analysis on parameters considered key or uncertain; standard errors / confidence intervals should be presented for population PK parameters.

uncertainty_reported: Multiple methods accepted; no statement on which method gives best-calibrated CIs. Calibration of intervals is not directly checked at regulatory review.
notes: FDA Population Pharmacokinetics final guidance (Feb 2022): parameter estimates should be presented with standard errors and/or CIs; bootstrap, SIR, log-likelihood profiling, or asymptotic SEs are acce

uncertainty_reported: Same uncertainty-reporting requirement; no calibration assessment built into the workflow.
notes: EMA pop-PK reporting guideline: CIs/SEs of population parameters should be reported because they indicate precision of estimates.

uncertainty_reported: Explicit endorsement of uncertainty propagation in simulation, but no normative test on whether the credible intervals are well-calibrated under the assumed model.
notes: ICH M15 (draft) on Model-Informed Drug Development: simulation methods should account for parameter and assumption uncertainties; introduces risk-based credibility framework.

uncertainty_reported: Monte Carlo on parameter and MIC uncertainty is the propagation mechanism. Model misspecification in the population PK model would directly bias PTA estimates and hence the breakpoint.
notes: EUCAST sets PK/PD breakpoints via Monte Carlo simulation incorporating population PK uncertainty and MIC distributions; the probability of target attainment (PTA) is the central decision metric. Break

uncertainty_reported: MIC variability is explicitly acknowledged as a 1-dilution band; ATU is a categorical warning, not a propagated probability distribution into dose decisions.
notes: Broth MIC tests are reproducible only to within +/- one doubling dilution. EUCAST introduced an "Area of Technical Uncertainty (ATU)" for known-difficult organism/agent combinations.

uncertainty_reported: Reporting flagged at the regulatory level; no formal CI is reported with the S/I/R call to the clinician.
notes: Documented real-world consequence: EUCAST formally warned that commercially available cefiderocol AST tests have "problems with accuracy, reproducibility, bias and/or skipped wells", meaning clinical

uncertainty_reported: Recent extensions implement bootstrap-based 95% prediction intervals (2.5/97.5 percentile of aggregated bootstrap predictions) - explicit acknowledgement that point predictions are insufficient.
notes: ComBase Predictor (USDA/UK/IFR) houses >60,000 records and uses Baranyi as the primary growth model; main outputs are point predictions of N(t).

uncertainty_reported: Bias quantified post-hoc; prediction intervals reported but the documented fail-dangerous rate is a direct stakes signal.
notes: Baranyi model is the de facto primary growth model in HACCP / shelf-life prediction. Lag-time predictions for Listeria in RTE meats showed 7% fail-dangerous bias (i.e. predicted less growth than actua

uncertainty_reported: Mandatory for EFSA scientific opinions but reliant on assessor's choice of method; calibration check is not required.
notes: EFSA Guidance on Uncertainty Analysis in Scientific Assessment: uncertainty must be expressed quantitatively where possible and incorporated in BIOHAZ panel scientific opinions. Variability and uncert

uncertainty_reported: Required at the international standard level but specific propagation methods left to the assessor.
notes: Codex Alimentarius CXG 30: microbiological risk assessment must include uncertainty and sensitivity analysis; growth modelling is an integral exposure-assessment input.

uncertainty_reported: Validation against CIs is explicit; ongoing uncertainty propagation into the safety decision is not built into the guideline.
notes: FDA HACCP guidelines: predictive models (including Baranyi-style growth models) can support critical-limit setting; model predictions should be compared against 95% confidence intervals during validat

uncertainty_reported: Outputs include fitted Baranyi/Gompertz parameters and (in IPMP 2013) some confidence intervals; downstream propagation into shelf-life is user-driven, not automatic.
notes: USDA Pathogen Modeling Program (PMP Online) and Integrated Pathogen Modeling Program (IPMP) are USDA-supplied tools for growth-curve fitting in regulatory contexts.

uncertainty_reported: Regulator openly admits prediction uncertainty is large enough to make confident shelf-life calls infeasible - direct real-world stakes.
notes: EFSA states that "for RTE foods that support L. monocytogenes growth, it is impossible to predict with a high degree of certainty that the level will or will not exceed 100 CFU/g during their shelf li

uncertainty_reported: Growth-rate uncertainty propagated through state estimators (extended Kalman filters, particle filters); some MPC formulations use Monte Carlo on parameter uncertainty.
notes: Soft sensors (off-gas, capacitance, oxygen-uptake-rate) estimate biomass and specific growth rate in real time for fed-batch fermentation including mAb-producing CHO cultures. These estimates feed PID

uncertainty_reported: Many production deployments operate on point estimates; confidence in the prediction is rarely surfaced to operators.
notes: MLOps soft sensors deployed in industrial-scale fed-batch fermentation use ML models that may not propagate parameter uncertainty at all.

uncertainty_reported: Encouraged at the framework level but with no specific requirement on growth-rate CI calibration.
notes: FDA PAT framework: process understanding requires characterizing variability in CPPs and KPIs; QbD principles encourage building uncertainty bounds into control strategies.

uncertainty_reported: Robust control methods exist but adoption in production bioprocess control is uneven.
notes: Classic batch-fermentation control under uncertainty literature; modeling parameter uncertainty has been a known issue for >30 years.

uncertainty_reported: Decisions to abort/discard a batch are based on parameter deviation thresholds, not on calibrated CIs.
notes: Golden batch anomaly detection in bioreactor fermentation: deviations from reference parameters trigger root-cause analyses for out-of-trend batches that may be discarded.

uncertainty_reported: Meta-analyses treat the dosing approach as a binary intervention; do not stratify by calibration quality of the underlying Bayesian models.
notes: AUC-guided (typically Bayesian) vancomycin TDM associated with lower nephrotoxicity (6.2%) vs trough-guided (17.0%); odds ratio for AKI 0.625.

uncertainty_reported: Outcomes assessed at the cohort level; whether individual-patient credible intervals were trustworthy is not separately validated.
notes: Recent prospective evaluations of Bayesian vancomycin software in pediatric ARC and ICU patients show improved target attainment vs conventional dosing.

uncertainty_reported: Bootstrap-based parameter uncertainty (200 resamples standard). Explicit acknowledgement that parametric CIs can be miscalibrated under non-Gaussian/multimodal parameter distributions.
notes: Pmetrics/NPAG and BestDose use nonparametric estimation, designed specifically to detect subpopulations and outliers that parametric methods miss. Argued to improve coverage in misspecified-prior scen

uncertainty_reported: Parameter uncertainty IS propagated via Monte Carlo (typically 10,000 iterations). However, the propagation assumes the parameter posterior is correctly characterized - if covariance matrix is miscalibrated (common, see SIR papers), PTA estimates are biased.
notes: Mouton & Vinks: Monte Carlo simulation maximizes population PK data to optimize antibiotic dosing in critically ill patients. Standard workflow: (1) population PK with parameter covariance, (2) MIC di

uncertainty_reported: Monte Carlo on parameter uncertainty drives the dosing simulation; calibration of CIs underlying the MCS not separately reported.
notes: Development of pop-PK models of total and free piperacillin in critically ill children for Monte Carlo simulations and MIPD. Direct application of parameter-uncertainty-propagated MCS to dose recommen

uncertainty_reported: Risk-based credibility framework explicitly ties stakes to required level of validation. Calibration of uncertainty intervals is not separately tested but is part of credibility assessment.
notes: ASME V&V 40-2018 "Assessing Credibility of Computational Modeling Through Verification and Validation: Application to Medical Devices" is referenced by ICH M15 draft as the credibility framework. Mode

uncertainty_reported: Field acknowledges miscalibration; few empirical coverage studies published.
notes: 30-year review of Baranyi & Roberts model. Documents that bias in growth-rate / lag estimates is the dominant source of error in shelf-life prediction; prediction-interval coverage is rarely validated

uncertainty_reported: Validates point predictions only - does not test whether reported 95% intervals achieve 95% coverage.
notes: Ross's accuracy factor (Af) and bias factor (Bf) are the standard validation metrics in predictive microbiology. EU FBR-EFSA-style "fail-dangerous" criterion: model predicts safer than reality.

uncertainty_reported: Direct documentation that the dominant pop-PK estimation tool's default uncertainty estimate is unreliable.
notes: Bauer review on NONMEM 7.4: covariance step failures are routine, especially with sparse data; bootstrap and SIR are workarounds but each can produce different CI widths for the same dataset.
